Monaco's Thomas Lemar is one of the best players in France and has the "right profile" to play for Arsenal, according to Robert Pires.

The Gunners have already had several offers for the 21-year-old turned down this summer, but Sky sources say Arsene Wenger still hopes to strike a deal to bring him to the Emirates Stadium.

Lemar helped Monaco to the Ligue 1 title last season, scoring nine goals and claiming 10 assists, and former Arsenal and France winger Pires believes his countryman would be an excellent addition.

"I love him as a player," Pires told Sky Sports. "For me, Thomas Lemar is one of the best French players there is. He can play on the left, he can play on the right, he can score goals and he can make assists."

Arsenal have already signed Alexandre Lacazette from Lyon in a deal that could rise to £52m, but Wenger is eyeing more attacking reinforcements as speculation surrounding Alexis Sanchez's future continues.

The Gunners boss says he has given up on signing Kylian Mbappe, but Pires feels he would be wise to continue his pursuit of Lemar as he aims to turn the Gunners into genuine title contenders next season.

"Lemar is the kind of player who can make a difference," said the former Invincible. "He has the right profile to play for Arsenal, definitely. I hope for him and for Arsenal that it happens."

Sanchez, meanwhile, says he wants to play in the Champions League but insists his future depends on Arsenal. Wenger is reluctant to sell him to Manchester City despite him entering the final year of his contract.

Serie A side Sampdoria have offered Arsenal £6m plus £1.5m in add-ons to sign Jack Wilshere. [@SkyKaveh] #afc

Firstly, Mathieu Debuchy. The Frenchman will join Nice for a minimal / no fee but his wages of around £70,000 p/w will be shifted.

Woijech Szczesny likely to be next out the door with a £10million move to Juventus all but done. Reportedly earned circa £65,000 p/w at #afc

Kieran Gibbs is another to be deemed surplus to requirements and looks likely to join West Brom for around £10m - wages of around £60k p/w.

Newcastle (who also have an interest in Gibbs) are interested in signing Carl Jenkinson. Minimal fee, but £45,000 p/w wages to be shifted.

Crystal Palace have bid £16million for Calum Chambers. Arsenal willing to let him go, would like to include a buy-back clause (£20k wage).

It also looks like the end of the road for Jack Wilshere. Several clubs interested, Sampdoria appear to have made a £7m bid (£90,000 wage).

Lucas Perez will be allowed to leave, several clubs looking at a loan deal, Arsenal favour a sale at around the £15m paid (£45k wages).

David Ospina also likely to leave in search of first team football. Fee around £3million and wages of around £40,000.

Seen that Debuchy to Nice has 'collapsed', but he will still leave so the point remains. Arsenal have no intention of including in squad.


If all of these leave, Arsenal will hope to raise in excess of £60million and perhaps more crucially, free up around £400,000 p/w in wages.

By Arsene Wenger’s own admission, the endless questions about his future in each of last season’s press conferences were something of a distraction.


That should change this term after the manager signed a new two-year contract extension, and he hopes the stability that offers will lead to a successful 2017/18 campaign.

“For me it will be a change because it was a bit eternal, a repetition of the press conferences about that,” Wenger told Arsenal Player. “Maybe I made a mistake and allowed that to happen because I didn’t commit early enough.

“I think at some stage I did not think it would be a handicap for my commitment and it was not. But it was a bit of a handicap because it created a climate of insecurity and a lack of clarity about my person that maybe did not help at some stage.

“I believe everyone is focused on the new season and giving absolutely the best, so overall that is what we want as we go into it.

“We basically had three seasons last year: the first part was very positive, the second part was very negative, and the third part was very positive. I believe it is important that we analyse well what happened to us, and where we can improve - and then transfer the optimistic final part of the season into the new campaign and start in a very strong way.”

The former Germany No 1 was known for his positive influence on the changing room during his playing days, and Wenger believes he can have a similar impact in his new role too.

“He has a demanding attitude and can give his desire to win to the players, to help them achieve their targets,” the manager told Arsenal Player. “That’s what you want from these players.

“He can also give good advice because he has gone through periods where it was not easy for him, where it wasn't all going well, and I believe his rigorous attitude to his career is similar to Per Mertesacker. It’s important that a team is surrounded by people like that.

“He enjoys being involved in training as well, and to experience the pleasure he had when he was giving his best for Arsenal Football Club again. He’s still highly motivated and that motivation never goes. When you want to do things well, I don’t think that ever changes.”

We have a new first-team coach this season, and it’s a face that will be familiar to Arsenal fans all over the world.

After making 200 appearances and winning two major trophies across two spells with the club, Jens Lehmann has returned to north London to assist Arsene Wenger and his coaching staff.

The former Germany No 1 was known for his positive influence on the changing room during his playing days, and Wenger believes he can have a similar impact in his new role too.

“He has a demanding attitude and can give his desire to win to the players, to help them achieve their targets,” the manager told Arsenal Player. “That’s what you want from these players. 

“He can also give good advice because he has gone through periods where it was not easy for him, where it wasn't all going well, and I believe his rigorous attitude to his career is similar to Per Mertesacker. It’s important that a team is surrounded by people like that.

“He enjoys being involved in training as well, and to experience the pleasure he had when he was giving his best for Arsenal Football Club again. He’s still highly motivated and that motivation never goes. When you want to do things well, I don’t think that ever changes.”

2016/2017球季基奧特在阿仙奴失去了正選位置，山齊士取代了正選「基佬」前鋒位置，基奧特今季大部分時間都在後備席，當上球隊「後備殺手」，成為Plan B 戰術。

雖然基奧特以後備奇兵登場，但是今季他仍交足貨，在各項賽事已入了16球，交出6個助攻。擔任後備仍交出此成績，需稱讚一下基佬。今季有幾次都是靠基奧特入波救了球隊，印象深刻如首循環作客曼聯，首循環一箭定江山贏西布朗，及補時絕殺追和般尼茅夫，就以三場比賽，基奧特已幫阿仙奴拿取7分，相當重要。

阿仙奴球迷一直抱怨球隊沒有世界級前鋒，在山齊士還未加盟兵工廠時，雲加會用基奧特擔當前鋒，雖然基奧特控球技術差一些，但身材高大的特點成為了基奧特優勢，可衝可撞。這類柱躉式前鋒在現今球壇愈來愈少，基奧特這類在球隊進攻時作支點，作用亦用於牽制對手，這就是阿仙奴之前打4-2-3-1陣式時的戰術。

2016/2017球季基奧特在阿仙奴失去了正選位置，山齊士取代了正選「基佬」前鋒位置，基奧特今季大部分時間都在後備席，當上球隊「後備殺手」，成為Plan B 戰術。

雖然基奧特以後備奇兵登場，但是今季他仍交足貨，在各項賽事已入了16球，交出6個助攻。擔任後備仍交出此成績，需稱讚一下基佬。今季有幾次都是靠基奧特入波救了球隊，印象深刻如首循環作客曼聯，首循環一箭定江山贏西布朗，及補時絕殺追和般尼茅夫，就以三場比賽，基奧特已幫阿仙奴拿取7分，相當重要。

阿仙奴球迷一直抱怨球隊沒有世界級前鋒，在山齊士還未加盟兵工廠時，雲加會用基奧特擔當前鋒，雖然基奧特控球技術差一些，但身材高大的特點成為了基奧特優勢，可衝可撞。這類柱躉式前鋒在現今球壇愈來愈少，基奧特這類在球隊進攻時作支點，作用亦用於牽制對手，這就是阿仙奴之前打4-2-3-1陣式時的戰術。
